overview: Gyan Bharatam is a national mission led by the Ministry of Culture to digitally preserve, catalog, and share India’s rich manuscript heritage with the world. It aims to safeguard and revitalize India’s Manuscript Heritage through systematic survey and documentation, scientific conservation, digitization, and publication, along with the creation of a national digital repository (NDR). The initiative was envisioned by the Honorable Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi, and the Hon’ble Finance Minister announced it in the Union Budget 2025–26. The Standing Finance Committee (SFC) has sanctioned Rs 491.66 crore for the period 2025–2031.
objectives: Safeguard and revitalize India’s Manuscript Heritage through systematic survey and documentation
Conserve manuscripts through scientific methods for preservation while training experts to care for them
Digitize and protect manuscripts with smart, sustainable technology for the future
Work with academicians & scholars to decode ancient manuscripts through translation and transliteration
Study and share knowledge of manuscripts through publication of critically edited manuscripts
Ensure accessibility of the National Digital Repository for research, education, and public engagement
eligibility: Open to individuals and institutions holding manuscripts, including citizen contributors, scholars, libraries, monasteries, temples, and academic institutions across India who wish to participate in the preservation, digitization, and sharing of manuscript heritage.
benefits: Access to digitized manuscripts for research, education, and public engagement; opportunity to register as a Gyan Bharatam Scholar; participation in workshops, conferences, and webinars; collaboration with researchers across India; access to manuscript repositories; contribution to national digital heritage infrastructure; recognition as part of a living network of seekers, scholars, teachers, and knowledge-keepers.
financial_support: The Standing Finance Committee (SFC) has sanctioned Rs 491.66 crore for the period 2025–2031 to support the operations of Gyan Bharatam, including survey, conservation, digitization, and creation of the National Digital Repository.
application_process: 1. Visit the Gyan Bharatam website at https://gyanbharatam.com/ 2. Navigate to the 'Register as Scholar' section via the homepage or community page 3. Fill out the scholar registration form with personal and institutional details 4. Submit the form to join the Gyan Bharatam Parivar network 5. Upon approval, gain access to manuscript repositories and collaborative opportunities.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
contact_details: Email: gyanbharatam2025@gmail.com | Phone: +91-11-23315625 | Shri Samar Nanda, Joint Secretary: +91 11 23386504 | Shri Inderjeet Singh, Director: +91 11 24014229 | Prof. Anirban Dash, Project Director: +91 11 23315625
caveats: Manuscript conservation and digitization efforts are subject to institutional partnerships and MoUs
Access to certain manuscript collections may be restricted based on custodian policies
Financial support is allocated for the period 2025–2031 and subject to government approvals
